key_insights.insight: The mind operates with rational and emotional circuitry, constantly influencing self-image and behavior.
key_insights.quote: You have this circuitry, which is rational and logical, looking at the facts, and you have a circuitry, which is emotionally based.

key_insights.insight: Self-image is influenced by both rational thought and emotional impulses, and they can create conflicting perceptions of oneself.
key_insights.quote: We get two images from two different circuits

key_insights.insight: Many drives and values adopted are not intrinsic but are influenced by the desire for social approval.
key_insights.quote: It feels like our drives and our values are somewhat handed to us and we don't even know that something isn't our true sort of intrinsic driver.

key_insights.insight: True drives are fundamental needs, while behaviors are often attached to the desire for social approval.
key_insights.quote: Drives are things like the need to eat,the need to have security. These are drives, the need to be a parent.

key_insights.insight: The desire for approval is rooted in the primal fear of being excluded from the group.
key_insights.quote: Gaining approval from people is actually based in the orbital frontal cortex,the chimp again, where it's terrified of being excluded from the troop.

key_insights.insight: The 'perfect person' exercise helps access your inherent character traits, revealing your authentic self.
key_insights.quote: If you had control of that part of your brain, which you have, and there was no interference from the rest of the brain, then that's exactly how you would be in life. That is you.

key_insights.insight: Interference from the 'chimp' part of the brain can lead to actions that contradict one's desired character traits.
Taking responsibility for actions influenced by the 'chimp' involves acknowledging mistakes and apologizing.
key_insights.quote: You're a hundred percent responsible for managing the chimp so you need an apology so when you know the chimp's been a bit brisk or rude you've got to stop and say I apologize.

key_insights.insight: Not everyone desires the same 'good' traits; psychopaths, for example, prioritize power and ego.
key_insights.quote: When you get to the psychopath they wouldn't put these and when i asked them they did not put honesty they did not put compassion they were not relevant they had a very different list of who they would ideally like to be and it wasn't pleasant it was all about power it was all about ego.

actionable_advice.advice: Write down the character traits of the perfect person you want to be.
actionable_advice.how_to: Get a blank piece of paper and list the qualities and traits that define your ideal self, excluding external drives.
Become aware of your immediate, impulsive reactions and take responsibility by apologizing for any inappropriate actions or communications resulting from the 'chimp' influence.

contrarian_claims: The 'human' part of a psychopath isn't necessarily good; the 'chimp' might be the nicer part.
Challenging the assumption that everyone wants to be compassionate and honest by using the example of how a psycopath doesn't value those character traits
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 27 - How To Become The Person You Want To Be: Prof. Steve Peters
